requirement_title: Server error during login is handled
title: EP: Database error propagated to Express error handler
precondition: The application is configured with an Express error handler
test_steps:
Simulate a database error during login (e.g., by disconnecting the database or using an invalid query).
Send a login request.
Observe the response and server logs.
test_data: Login request that triggers a Sequelize database error (e.g., with a malformed SQL query or after shutting down the database)
expected_result: The error is caught by Sequelize and passed to the Express error handler, which sends an appropriate 500 Internal Server Error response without leaking sensitive details.

requirement_title: Server error during login is handled
title: DT: Database error triggers propagation to error handler
precondition: The application is configured with an Express error handler
test_steps:
Force a database error condition during login (e.g., by causing a Sequelize error).
Verify that the error is passed to the Express error handler.
Check the response status and body.
test_data: Any login request that results in a database error (e.g., due to a database connection failure)
expected_result: The Express error handler is invoked; response is a 500 error with a generic message, no stack trace leaked.

requirement_title: Server error during login is handled
title: State transition path 1 (ALL_TRANSITIONS)
expected_result: The error is propagated to the Express error handler.
coverage_items: Processing--errorDetected[Database or Sequelize error occurs]/Propagate the error to the Express error handler-->ErrorHandling
